theory.canonical_statement: ID3406 records Em Rotational Or Radial Operator as a source-extracted canonical equation.
theory.technical_interpretation: The entry preserves the equation exactly as extracted from `D:\00_Engine\CSV\00_Canon_00_TRAWIN_definition.tex` for registry alignment and later derivation.

latex_blocks: \[0.4em] $\mathsf{R}$ & Rotational or radial operator & $\nabla \times,\; r\hat{\mathbf{r}}$ \\[0.4em] $\mathsf{A}$ & Amplitude / acceleration field & $A(x,t)$ \\[0.4em] $\mathsf{W}$ & Wave or propagation operator & $\nabla^2 - \frac{1}{c^2}\partial_t^2$ \\[0.6em] $\mathsf{I}$ & Integration / information aggregation & $\int \rho(x,t)\, dV$ \\[0.6em] $\mathsf{N}$ & Normalization / closure operator & $\mathcal{N}^{-1}\sum_i,\;\; \mathbf{n}\cdot\mathbf{F}=0$ \\ \hline \end{tabular} \end{center} \begin{remark}[Compositional closure] \label{rem:trawin_closure} Expressions formed by ordered composition of elements of $\mathcal{O}_{\mathrm{TRAWIN}}$ are interpreted as \emph{closed} when their actio ...
